patterns.label: ADO Notification Triage
patterns.llmRationale: Azure DevOps work-item notifications follow a rigid subject-line pattern (Task/Bug/Requirement/Feature/Epic + numeric ID) trivially parseable into five sub-classifiers; at freq=30/week this is the single highest-volume rule-based routing opportunity in the dataset.

patterns.label: Recurring Meeting Agenda Generator
patterns.llmRationale: Six distinct weekly recurring meeting types plus six stakeholder 1:1s all follow fixed attendee sets and predictable agenda structures; a skill pre-populating agendas from prior meeting notes and calendar metadata could eliminate 20-30 min of prep per slot.

patterns.label: Meeting Notes & Action Item Capture
patterns.llmRationale: A stable Loop doc structure (Agenda to Notes to Decisions/Open Questions to Follow-up Tasks) is reproduced verbatim across 4 meeting instances and 4 document instances each week; the schema is fixed enough for a skill to auto-populate all sections from a meeting transcript.

patterns.label: Eval Coaching & Scoping
patterns.llmRationale: PM is the sole DRI answering 15 recurring question types about eval methodology, metric selection, and tooling bugs (CLI/CSV/feature-flags) this cycle; the T1/T2/T3 decomposition is validated, and a skill trained on the eval playbook and tool docs could intercept the majority of FAQ-level requests.

patterns.label: Access & Governance Alert Classifier
patterns.llmRationale: Security and compliance alerts from Purview, SharePoint, and Loop follow consistent sender patterns and require a binary classify-and-route response; freq=7 makes this a high-priority candidate for automated triage with defined escalation paths.

patterns.label: Post-Meeting External Follow-Up Drafter
patterns.llmRationale: Three distinct urgency-level emails (standard follow-up, urgent Declarative Agent grounding escalation, administrative university escalation) all require tone calibration; a skill with urgency-tier templates and action-item extraction would cover all sub-types reliably.

patterns.label: Parallel Eval Artifact Creation
patterns.llmRationale: EvalCon planning threads and Agent Eval Sets template maintenance show 3+ people independently recreating structurally identical eval artifacts; a canonical template scaffolder locking the schema and pre-populating context would eliminate duplication overhead explicitly flagged by the team.

patterns.label: Daily Briefing Generator
patterns.llmRationale: Five date-stamped planning files ([Mar 19] Help me prepare for my day) confirm a daily structured prep routine; a skill pulling calendar, high-priority emails, and open action items into a structured morning briefing would fully automate this workflow.

patterns.label: Broadcast & Digest Email Classifier
patterns.llmRationale: Viva Engage daily/weekly digests and community announcements are passive inbox noise with zero reply obligation; a classifier surfacing only actionable items eliminates daily scroll time with near-zero misclassification risk given rigid sender and subject patterns.

patterns.label: JSON Config & Agent State Authoring
patterns.llmRationale: Skill-detector ZIP packages containing SKILL.md specs, shell scripts, and .json config files are produced repeatedly in short intervals; schema-driven JSON generation is a core LLM competency and a skill template for agent state files would eliminate the boilerplate authoring loop.

patterns.label: Training Event Artifact Coordinator
patterns.llmRationale: Camp AIR consumed 900 minutes across two full days plus adjacent EvalCon planning threads (175min Learning Fridays); a skill coordinating pre-event logistics, session note templates, hackathon standups, and post-event synthesis would reclaim significant PM bandwidth at multi-day scale.

patterns.label: Partner & Customer Eval Enablement Meetings
patterns.llmRationale: Six external sessions across 50+ partner domains (Accenture, HCL, EY, KPMG, Avanade, Cognizant) consumed 13 hours this cycle; EDII 5.0+ confirmed. A skill auto-generating per-partner eval readiness packs and scoping questionnaires would compress pre-meeting prep substantially across all external engagements.

patterns.label: Cross-Tool Context Fragmentation
patterns.llmRationale: Technical artifact distribution via Teams (GitHub repos, ZIPs, YAML, Loop pages, aka.ms links) at freq=8 shows PM acting as a manual context-bundling layer; a skill packaging related artifacts with context and recipient-appropriate summaries would reduce the back-and-forth ask-for-link cycle.

patterns.label: VoC Exec Summary Drafter
patterns.llmRationale: Cross-org VOC routing requests (DRI ownership, ADO area path, eval feedback destination) hit PM at freq=4 from Field Engineers, MCS Analytics, and PG; a skill mapping incoming feedback to DRI, ADO path, and executive one-liner template would offload the routing layer entirely.

patterns.label: Skills Discovery & Workflow Automation
patterns.llmRationale: Agent architecture and MCP/CLI scoping discussions (skill-creator, skill-detector, PRD generator, WorkIQ integration, Copilot Studio gaps) at freq=5 confirm an active skills-library design loop; a discovery skill surfacing categorized workflow candidates would bootstrap the library faster.

patterns.label: Redundant Deck Creation
patterns.llmRationale: Three parallel eval enablement decks (customer intro, pitch, enablement, summit) with overlapping content were created by different team members in the same week; a skill detecting structural overlap and generating delta-only content from a canonical master deck would eliminate this waste.

patterns.label: Weekly Status Report Generation
patterns.llmRationale: Weekly done/next/blockers updates were requested in multiple parallel formats (weekly sync, bi-weekly newsletter, Cape Day LT slides), forcing teams to restate progress redundantly; a skill generating all format variants from a single status entry would collapse this overhead.

patterns.label: Skill Library Socialization
patterns.llmRationale: Skilluminator dashboard HTML, skill-detector ZIP packages, and SKILL.md specs are produced and reshared across chats repeatedly; the socialization overhead implies the library infrastructure itself needs a discovery and onboarding skill to reduce friction for new contributors.

patterns.label: Solution Library Pipeline Ops
patterns.llmRationale: Weekly Solution Library Check-In (Mondays 9:05-9:30 AM) confirmed active again this cycle; rising trend reflects pattern returning from dormancy and must be confirmed for a second consecutive cycle before raising the automationThreshold.

patterns.label: CAPE Evals Strategy Thread Synthesis
patterns.llmRationale: Long cross-org email threads (Project Crucible Evals-First Mindset, customer donated evals playbook, Declarative Agent grounding escalation) plus EvalCon planning discussions require PM to synthesize multi-stakeholder context into decision-ready summaries; EvalCon deadline amplification is active.

candidateSkills.name: ado-notification-router
candidateSkills.description: Parses Azure DevOps work-item email notifications by type (Task/Bug/Requirement/Feature/Epic + ID), classifies urgency and team ownership, and routes to the correct queue or drafts a triage summary — eliminating manual inbox scanning.
triggerExamples: I'm getting 30+ ADO notification emails per week and spending 45 minutes just triaging them
Route my Azure DevOps notification emails by work item type and assignee
Help me triage ADO eval and build notifications into a prioritized list
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates 45+ min/week of rule-based inbox processing at freq=30 with near-zero misclassification risk; subject-line grammar is rigid and deterministic across Task, Bug, Requirement, Feature, and Epic types.

candidateSkills.name: recurring-agenda-generator
candidateSkills.description: Pre-populates meeting agendas for recurring syncs by pulling prior meeting notes, open action items, and calendar metadata — producing a ready-to-use agenda draft 30 minutes before each meeting starts.
triggerExamples: Generate an agenda for my Monday Project Crucible v-team sync based on last week's notes
Prepare the recurring Evals Sync agenda with outstanding action items
Create a standing 1:1 agenda for my stakeholder check-in this afternoon
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates 20-30 min of agenda prep per meeting slot across 6+ weekly recurring syncs and 6 1:1s, saving 2-3 hours/week of meeting overhead for the entire team.

candidateSkills.name: meeting-notes-action-extractor
candidateSkills.description: Generates a structured Loop meeting notes doc from a transcript or voice memo — auto-populating Agenda, Meeting Notes, Decisions/Open Questions, and Follow-up Tasks sections with named owners and due dates.
triggerExamples: Turn this meeting transcript into a Loop doc with action items and owners
Capture the decisions and follow-ups from today's evals sync
Create a post-meeting summary with open questions and assigned next steps
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Automates a 4x/week manual workflow with a fixed schema; reduces note-capture time from 15-20 min per meeting to under 2 min for review-and-send. SGI 92 — structural gap confirmed across the whole team.

candidateSkills.name: eval-design-advisor
candidateSkills.description: Answers recurring eval methodology questions (scope, metric selection, coverage, tooling, CLI usage, feature flags) using the eval playbook and tool documentation — intercepting FAQ-level inbound requests before they reach the sole expert DRI.
triggerExamples: How many eval cases are enough for a new Copilot agent feature?
What metrics should I use for groundedness vs. tool_call_accuracy in my eval set?
Help me troubleshoot this CSV import error in the eval CLI
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Offloads 15 inbound requests/cycle from a single DRI (BSI 81 CRITICAL); unblocks field engineers, PMs, and designers who currently wait in a queue for expert guidance across 10+ distinct question types.

candidateSkills.name: access-alert-classifier
candidateSkills.description: Classifies incoming security and compliance alerts from Purview, SharePoint, and Loop — tagging each as Action-Required, Informational, or False-Positive and routing to the correct escalation path or dismissing safely.
triggerExamples: Classify this batch of Purview and Loop compliance alerts by urgency
Which of these security emails actually need my action today?
Triage my Office365 admin alerts and tell me what I need to do
candidateSkills.valueProposition: 7+ alerts/week with consistent sender patterns and binary classify-and-route logic; near-zero creative judgment required makes this one of the highest-confidence automation candidates in the dataset.
